book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice.
The Canons of the CCXVII Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage. Commonly Called The Code of Canons of the African Church.

title: Canon XXIII.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: That bishops shall not go across seas.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Item, That bishops shall not go beyond seas without consulting the bishop of the primatial see of his own province: so that from him they may be able to receive a formed or commendatory letter.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: A bishop is not to cross the seas unless he has received from the Primate of his region a letter dimissory.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: This is Canon xxvij. of Hippo, 393.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

verses.text: [The use of Letters Commendatory was very early in the Church; St. Paul mentions them II.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: And it is not easy to be conceived how discipline can be restored but by the reviving of this practice.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It is surely irregular to admit all chance comers to the Communion, who, for aught we know, may stand excommunicated by their own bishop.
